frameworks.name: neuro-reinforcement (decoded neurofeedback / DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: Implicit multi-voxel pattern reinforcement targeting category-specific representations

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Intrinsic network coupling indexed by group ICA as mechanism readout

frameworks.name: amygdala reactivity model of phobia
frameworks.one_line: Threat-response circuitry framed as treatment target

core_question: Do multi-voxel neuro-reinforcement effects on amygdala reactivity in phobia map onto changes in resting-state functional connectivity?
key_assumptions: Implicit reinforcement of multi-voxel patterns can shift downstream affective responses
Group ICA recovers a stable resting network across pre/post sessions
Untargeted phobic animal serves as within-subject placebo control
manipulations: Targeted vs untargeted phobic-animal category (double-blind, within-subject)
key_findings: A visual-network-overlapping resting-state component recurred pre and post
Connectivity in higher-level visual and cognitive regions of this network increased post-treatment
Greater connectivity increases predicted greater decreases in amygdala reactivity to targeted phobic stimuli
interpretations: Multi-voxel neuro-reinforcement induces persisting connectivity changes beyond the targeted region
Connectivity shifts in visual/cognitive cortex may mediate downstream amygdala attenuation
